title: Magnistipula tessmannii
taxonomy: Magnistipula tessmannii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Chrysobalanaceae. This family is commonly known as the cocoa or cabuceo family. The genus Magnistipula is a group of trees and shrubs native to tropical Africa. Magnistipula tessmannii is native to the tropical forests of Central Africa. It is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms.

intro: Magnistipula tessmannii, also known as the African bushwillow, is a plant species belonging to the Combretaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of M. tessmannii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
